key_points: Elon Musk's xAI filed a federal lawsuit challenging Colorado's AI regulation law
xAI argues the law violates the First Amendment by compelling AI companies to alter model outputs
Colorado was the first US state to pass broad AI regulatory measures targeting algorithmic discrimination
The law requires impact assessments and user appeal rights in high-risk AI decisions (hiring, lending, admissions)
The case could set a precedent for First Amendment defences against AI anti-discrimination laws
reference_summary: Elon Musk's xAI sued Colorado over its landmark AI regulation law, arguing it violates the First Amendment by forcing companies to alter model outputs to meet anti-discrimination standards. Colorado was the first state to pass such broad AI rules, requiring impact assessments and appeal rights in hiring, lending, and school admissions decisions.
